trigger: User reports 'workspace goes missing' or 'something keeps killing the workspace' for cmux Claude panels
rule: Check ~/Library/Logs/DiagnosticReports/*.ips for zsh / node crashes with parentProc/responsibleProc=cmux. zsh on macOS 26.x has SIGSEGV crashes in killjb/waitjobs at child-side-of-fork-pre-exec, especially when ~/.zprofile contains heavy shell functions that recurse (nvm use, brew shellenv, scout git_hook). When zsh dies during cmux's auto-relaunch of claude, the cmux pane has nothing left to render → the workspace 'looks missing'. Fix path: (a) switch login shell to /opt/homebrew/bin/zsh (newer); (b) move nvm use from .zprofile to .zshrc; (c) wrap cmux launchCommand in 'bash -lc ...' so bash takes over if zsh crashes. The cmux workspace itself is never destroyed — it's the inner shell that's segfaulting.
why: On 2026-05-22 Mukul: 'I've been manually starting Claude, because the workspace goes missing.' Investigation found zsh SIGSEGV at 19:09:10 (parentProc=zsh, responsibleProc=cmux) with stack trace recursing through runshfunc/doshfunc/execlist 7+ levels deep, faulting in killjb. Two node crashes today also under cmux. The workspace UUID never changed; the inner zsh process did. Apple Silicon + macOS 26 + heavy .zprofile = unstable shell init under cmux's fork-exec pattern.
